skill.name: goal-setter
skill.description: Transform vague objectives into structured, measurable goals with success criteria, subgoals, milestones, dependencies, and LeanOS canvas links.
skill.summary: Turn user objectives into actionable goal plans with metrics, milestones, and workspace goal files.
skill.author: BellaBe

security_audit.summary: AI review found the static high-risk detections to be false positives caused by Markdown code fences, inline file names, planning terms, and business acronyms. No evidence found of executable commands, credential access, prompt injection, malware intent, or data exfiltration. The only residual risk is intended workspace file access for reading LeanOS strategy files and creating goal documents.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Detections Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The cited backtick detections are Markdown code fences, inline file paths, tables, and examples. No evidence found of Ruby backtick execution, shell invocation, command construction, or executable script content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are Markdown formatting and example text, not executable Ruby or shell syntax. The skill consists of one Markdown file and contains no scripts or command runners.

low_findings.title: Static Sensitive, Crypto, And Reconnaissance Detections Are Terminology Matches
low_findings.description: The Windows SAM finding is the business acronym in TAM/SAM/SOM. The weak-crypto and reconnaissance hits are ordinary planning words, goal examples, file references, or business metrics, with no credential access, hash use, system probing, or network scanning behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line review shows business planning content and schema examples rather than security-sensitive APIs or commands. No evidence found of malicious intent or hidden exfiltration behavior.

low_findings.title: Controlled Workspace File Access Is Part Of Intended Workflow
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to read LeanOS canvas and active-goal files, then create a goal Markdown file under strategy/goals/active. This is a legitimate capability, but users should review proposed file changes before applying them in sensitive workspaces.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem behavior is explicitly documented and limited to project planning files. It is not malicious, but it is a real capability that can read and modify workspace content.

content.value_statement: Ambitious objectives often fail because success, scope, and next steps are unclear. This skill converts an objective into a measurable goal with milestones, subgoals, dependencies, and LeanOS canvas links.
actual_capabilities: Extracts objective, motivation, deadline, constraints, and success signals from user input.
Classifies goals as business, brand, product, learning, or custom based on context.
Defines measurable success criteria, subgoals, milestones, dependencies, and autonomy level.
Uses LeanOS canvas sections to align goals with assumptions, metrics, constraints, and strategy.
Creates a structured Markdown goal file for strategy/goals/active when the user confirms the plan.
Flags vague objectives, missing deadlines, ambiguous success criteria, and overlapping active goals.
limitations: Requires a deadline or milestone target before a complete goal can be created.
Works best in repositories that already use the LeanOS strategy/canvas and strategy/goals structure.
Does not execute the plan or complete subgoals by itself.
Needs user confirmation before creating high-impact or potentially duplicate goals.

use_cases.title: Founder Revenue Goal Planning
use_cases.description: Turn a revenue target into measurable criteria, acquisition subgoals, economics checks, and milestone dates.

use_cases.title: Product Launch Structuring
use_cases.description: Convert a launch intent into scoped product outcomes, dependencies, beta milestones, and completion criteria.

use_cases.title: Personal Learning Roadmap
use_cases.description: Define a skill-building goal with proof of competence, practice milestones, and progress tracking.

prompt_templates.title: Create A Basic Goal
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to achieve [objective] by [date]. Help me turn it into a structured goal with success criteria and milestones.

prompt_templates.title: Refine A Vague Objective
prompt_templates.prompt: My goal is [rough objective], but it is still vague. Ask only the questions needed to make it measurable and actionable.

prompt_templates.title: Build A Strategy-Aligned Goal
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the relevant LeanOS canvas sections to create a goal for [objective]. Link assumptions, constraints, metrics, and affected canvas sections.

prompt_templates.title: Audit And Merge Goal Overlap
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my active goals for overlap with [new objective]. Recommend whether to merge, replace, split, or keep the new goal separate.

output_examples.input: I want to reach 50,000 dollars in monthly recurring revenue by the end of Q2.
output: A business goal with revenue, customer count, profit, and payback success criteria.
Subgoals for closing customers, validating unit economics, and scaling acquisition.
Milestones at 25 percent, 50 percent, 75 percent, and final target progress.

output_examples.input: Ship the mobile app by March 31.
output: A product goal tied to solution scope, user problem, constraints, and launch criteria.
Subgoals for core features, beta testing, and store submission.
Dependencies and measurable outcomes such as beta users, crash rate, and release status.

output_examples.input: Build my LinkedIn presence for thought leadership.
output: A brand goal with audience, content, reach, and lead-generation success criteria.
Subgoals for content pillars, publishing cadence, and audience engagement.
Canvas links to positioning, customer segments, and growth assumptions.

best_practices: Provide a deadline, target metric, current baseline, and important constraints in the first request.
Review the proposed success criteria before allowing the skill to create a goal file.
Keep canvas assumptions current so new goals link to accurate strategy context.
anti_patterns: Do not use it for open-ended wishes without a deadline or measurable outcome.
Do not skip duplicate-goal checks when several active goals already exist.
Do not set high-impact goals to full autonomy without reviewing the plan first.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates a structured goal plan with objective, success criteria, subgoals, milestones, dependencies, state, and canvas links.

faq.question: Does it require LeanOS?
faq.answer: It is designed for LeanOS, but the planning method can still help when the same strategy folders are absent.

faq.question: Can it create files?
faq.answer: Yes. When confirmed, it can create a Markdown goal file under strategy/goals/active in the workspace.

faq.question: How does it handle vague goals?
faq.answer: It asks for missing success criteria, deadline, constraints, or goal type before producing a final structure.

faq.question: What goal types are supported?
faq.answer: It supports business, brand, product, learning, and custom goals.

faq.question: Does it execute the goal plan?
faq.answer: No. It structures the goal and links future work, but separate execution threads complete the actual tasks.
